summary: Yegge reviews Borderlands 2 as a masterpiece undermined by a single fatal flaw: insufficient storage for the game's 87 bazillion guns. Despite praising the writing, humor, voice acting, balance, and visual style as capital-G Great, he argues Gearbox fundamentally misunderstands their own token economy. The game creates gun collectors but gives them no way to collect guns, with only about 12 storage slots for an endgame built around farming legendary weapons. He predicts players will drift back to Borderlands 1 or resort to save-file modding, and calls for Gearbox to implement a proper gun database system rather than arbitrary inventory limits.
key_points: Borderlands 2 is a great game with outstanding writing, humor, balance, and the best bandit dialogue in gaming, but inventory management is a serious step backwards
The 87 bazillion guns marketing is meaningless when players can only keep about 12 of them - Gearbox created a collector's game without collection infrastructure
The game feels like a theme park with inconsistent visual design - too many saturated colors and disparate themed areas lacking cohesive vision
Eridium currency has no meaningful sink while players desperately need more bank slots - an obvious missed opportunity
True endgame farming requires a database-like system to manage hundreds or thousands of guns, not the current primitive inventory
money_quotes: Of course, the whole point of 87 Bazillion Guns is LOST, if you don't let people keep them!
They're the neighborhood kid who catches a lizard and thinks it's really cool, and it *is* cool, except he puts it in a box and it dies.

It's actually a great test of whether you're an idiot, because if you don't like it or it doesn't appeal to you... well, you might not be an idiot per se; there's bound to be some explanation... I guess.
Any three colors go together, and any more than that looks like a peacock shitting rainbows.
Crazed psycho bandits running at you screaming that Pluto is still a planet, or complaining about how goddamn cold it is outside, or reciting Hamlet... it just never gets old.
Gearbox has created a gun-collector's game, but they haven't given us a way to collect guns. How messed up is that?
Modding is stupid, stupid, stupid; it's 12 year olds advertising that they are, in fact, mentally and emotionally really twelve years old.
key_insight: Creating a game centered on collecting 87 bazillion guns while only allowing players to keep a dozen of them reveals a fundamental misunderstanding of your own game's core appeal.
